title: Block 44 — EventsPage auto-filters by currentSport from SportContext

requirements.text: In EventsPage.tsx, import useEffect and useState from React, and import useSport from the SportContext. Consume sportStatus and currentSport from useSport().

quotes: Import: import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; import { useSport } from '../context/SportContext'; Inside component: const { sportStatus, currentSport } = useSport();

acceptanceCriteria: EventsPage imports useEffect, useState, and useSport
The component calls useSport() and destructures sportStatus and currentSport

requirements.text: In EventsPage.tsx, keep the existing selectedSport state (defaulting to 'All') and add a useEffect that sets selectedSport to currentSport when sportStatus === 'active' && currentSport is truthy, otherwise resets selectedSport to 'All'. The effect must depend on [sportStatus, currentSport].

quotes: useEffect(() => { if (sportStatus === 'active' && currentSport) { setSelectedSport(currentSport); } else { setSelectedSport('All'); } }, [sportStatus, currentSport]);

acceptanceCriteria: When sportStatus becomes 'active' and currentSport is set, selectedSport automatically updates to match currentSport
When sportStatus is 'inactive' or currentSport is falsy, selectedSport resets to 'All'
The useEffect re-runs whenever sportStatus or currentSport changes

requirements.text: In EventsPage.tsx, derive a filteredEvents list from the events array: when selectedSport === 'All', filteredEvents equals the full events array; otherwise, filteredEvents is events filtered to those where event.sport === selectedSport. Render filteredEvents in place of the raw events array.

quotes: const filteredEvents = selectedSport === 'All' ? events : events.filter(e => e.sport === selectedSport); Render filteredEvents instead of events.

acceptanceCriteria: When selectedSport is 'All', all events are displayed
When selectedSport is a specific sport (e.g. 'Tennis'), only events with event.sport === 'Tennis' are displayed
The event list UI renders from filteredEvents, not the raw events array

requirements.text: Verify end-to-end behavior: (1) deactivating a sport resets the Events list to show all events; (2) activating a sport (e.g. Tennis) causes the Events list to automatically show only events for that sport; (3) manually selecting 'All' via the filter chips still shows all events; (4) deactivating after a manual selection keeps the list at 'All'.

quotes: 1) Deactivate sport → Events shows All. 2) Activate Tennis → Events auto switches to Tennis (only tennis events). 3) Tap 'All' manually → should still show all. 4) Deactivate → stays All.

acceptanceCriteria: Deactivating sport shows all events in EventsPage
Activating a sport auto-filters EventsPage to only that sport's events
Tapping the 'All' filter chip manually shows all events regardless of sport status
Deactivating sport after any filter state resets display to all events

constraints: Do not modify SportContext.tsx — only consume it from EventsPage
Preserve the existing manual filter chip UI in EventsPage; the useEffect should drive the selected state but not remove user control
nonGoals: Changes to MapPage sport filtering (already implemented in Block 43)
